articles.title: A Snake as Long as a School Bus? Meet Vasuki, the 50-Foot Giant!
articles.summary: Imagine a snake longer than a school bus! Scientists found fossils of Vasuki indicus in India. This 47-million-year-old snake may have been 50 feet long, as big as Titanoboa.

articles.title: Smart Underwear Tracks Your Toots — Science Finally Answers: How Many Farts a Day Is Normal?
articles.summary: Scientists made a tiny sensor that snaps into underwear to count farts. In a test, people averaged 32 toots a day. Some farted only 4 times, others 59! Now they want to find the normal range.

articles.title: This Tiny Drone Navigates Like a Bat — Even in Fog and Smoke!
articles.summary: A palm-sized drone uses echolocation, just like a bat, to fly through dark, smoky places. It costs $400 and could help find people trapped in disasters.
